description_long: This is a rustic wooden picket fence composed of vertical slats in varying shades of brown, gray, and white, giving it a weathered, reclaimed appearance. The fence features two thick, square posts at each end, supporting two horizontal rails that hold the vertical pickets in place. Each picket has a pointed top, contributing to a classic, traditional look often seen in suburban or park settings. The wood grain and texture are visible, with some slats appearing more aged or distressed than others, adding to the visual interest. The construction is simple and functional, with no visible decorative embellishments or hardware. This type of fence is typically used to demarcate property boundaries or enclose small gardens and yards in urban or residential environments.
description: A rustic wooden picket fence with pointed vertical slats in mixed weathered tones, supported by two thick posts and horizontal rails. The design is simple, functional, and visually varied, suitable for urban or residential boundaries.
description_view_0: Front view showing all vertical pickets in alternating colors and the two horizontal rails connecting the posts.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thickness of the end post and the narrow profile of the fence.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with visible pickets and rails, but less color variation apparent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other end post and the slim profile of the fence.
